seo.title: AI Agent Hallucination Detection in Llama Stack Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent hallucination detection in Llama Stack agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Llama Stack agents, hallucinations manifest as fabricated tool outputs, incorrect memory retrievals, or safety shield false positives that derail agent reasoning. These issues arise from the framework's modular design where Llama models generate responses across safety, memory, and tool interfaces, often producing inconsistent or invented data. Detecting them requires inspecting agent turn responses against expected schemas and memory associations.

howItManifests.scenario: Safety shield filtering producing false positives on benign content
howItManifests.symptom: Agent rejects valid tool calls or responses labeled as 'unsafe' despite no violations
howItManifests.impact: Premature termination of agent loops, preventing task completion

howItManifests.scenario: Agentic loop turn limits exceeded without meaningful resolution
howItManifests.symptom: Agent generates repetitive or off-topic reasoning without converging on answers
howItManifests.impact: Timeout failures and incomplete task execution

howItManifests.scenario: Tool runtime responses not conforming to expected schema
howItManifests.symptom: Tools return malformed JSON or hallucinated fields not in Pydantic schema

howItManifests.scenario: Memory bank retrieval hallucinating document associations
howItManifests.symptom: Agent cites non-existent memory entries or wrong document IDs
howItManifests.impact: Factually incorrect responses based on fabricated context

detection.name: Safety Shield False Positive Check
detection.description: Parse Llama Stack safety response for false rejections on benign content

detection.name: Tool Schema Conformance Detector
detection.description: Validate tool responses against Llama Stack expected Pydantic schemas

detection.name: Memory Association Hallucination Check
detection.description: Verify cited memory document IDs exist in Llama Stack memory bank

fixes.name: Safety Shield Whitelist
fixes.description: Configure Llama Stack safety shield with domain-specific whitelists

fixes.name: Structured Tool Response Enforcement
fixes.description: Wrap Llama Stack tool calls with strict JSON schema enforcement

fixes.name: Memory Citation Validation Guard
fixes.description: Add pre-response check for valid memory associations in Llama Stack
fixes.code:
def validate_memory_citations(response, memory_bank):
    citations = response.get('citations', [])
    valid_citations = [c for c in citations if c in memory_bank]
    response['citations'] = valid_citations
    if len(valid_citations) < len(citations):
        response['warning'] = 'Removed invalid memory citations'
    return response

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Hallucination Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates seamlessly with Llama Stack by wrapping agent turns in vex.verify() post-processing, automatically detecting hallucinations across safety shield outputs, tool responses, and memory retrievals. It runs parallel checks against predefined test scenarios and Llama Stack schemas, flagging issues like false positive safety blocks or schema-violating tool outputs before they impact production agents.

The platform provides remediation templates specific to Llama Stack's APIs, auto-generating safety whitelists and tool schema fixes. Vex's continuous monitoring catches hallucination patterns across agent deployments, with one-click rollout of verified fixes maintaining agent reliability at scale.

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install Vex SDK today and eliminate Llama Stack hallucinations with one line of code.
